summary: The session focused on the differences between group bookings and allotment bookings in airline reservations. The user sought clarification on the distinct processes involved in each type of booking.
facts: Group bookings involve a representative contacting the airline directly to negotiate terms based on group size and travel dates. Allotment bookings require a travel agency to negotiate a fixed number of seats and rates with the airline in advance. Group bookings offer discounted fares and special perks for large groups traveling together. Allotment bookings allow unsold seats to be returned to the airline before a specified deadline without penalties.
